# Figure description: 1_Геологическая_карта_M-42-VIII.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-VIII/1_Геологическая_карта_M-42-VIII.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite figure** consisting of three distinct components:
1. A **regional geological map** (верхняя часть / upper portion) at 1:200,000 scale
2. Three **geophysical and geological cross-sections** (разрезы) shown below the map, each combining gravity/magnetic anomaly curves with a subsurface geological interpretation panel
3. A **supplementary index/location schema** and legend panel on the right margin

---

## Coverage and Subject

**Map Sheet:** М-42-VIII (shown prominently in the title block), part of the broader M-42 and N-42 regional survey coverage in **northern Kazakhstan**.

**Organization:** Министерство геологии Казахской ССР, Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR, Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association). The map was compiled by the Central Geological Prospecting Expedition.

**Authors listed:** Смольянова Е.М., Филатова Г.В.; scientific supervisors Наумкин В.Н., Гурусов А.М.

**Year:** 1984 (visible in margin)

**Cross-section lines:**
- **Разрез по линии З-Н** (Section along line Z-N) — upper cross-section
- **Разрез по линии Л-М** (Section along line L-M) — middle cross-section
- **Разрез по линии П-Р** (Section along line P-R) — lower cross-section

**Scale:** 1:200,000; contour interval 40 metres (Сплошные горизонтали проведены через 40 метров)

**Area:** Площадь 5208.4 км² (area of coverage stated in right margin legend block)

---

## Key Content

### Geological Map (Upper Panel)
The map displays a complex, multi-coloured geological pattern typical of a Paleozoic terrane in the Central Kazakhstan fold belt:

- **Dominant colours:**
  - **Yellow/pale ochre** — extensive areas likely representing Paleozoic (possibly Devonian or Carboniferous) sedimentary or volcanic-sedimentary sequences; occupy the central and western portions of the map
  - **Orange/brown tones (hatched and plain)** — prevalent in the eastern and northeastern sectors; likely represent granitic intrusions or metamorphic complexes, with foliation/lineation symbols suggesting structural fabric
  - **Pink/red patches** — scattered across the map, possibly representing younger granitic intrusions (often Permian granitoids in this region)
  - **Blue/light blue linear features** — river drainage networks and possibly dyke swarms or fault zones
  - **Green patches** — scattered, possibly representing specific volcanic units or alteration zones
  - **White areas** — likely Quaternary cover or alluvial deposits along drainage systems

- **Structural features:**
  - Prominent **fold traces and strike-dip symbols** especially in the eastern half, indicating a well-developed structural fabric with NW-SE trending fold axes
  - Multiple **fault lines** (shown as bold black lines, some with displacement indicators)
  - **Concentric patterns** in the south-central area suggesting a **domal structure or circular intrusion** (possibly a granite pluton with concentric aureole zonation)
  - Linear **dyke swarms** visible cutting across older units

- **Geophysical symbols:** Various borehole/drill hole locations marked with standard Soviet geological survey symbols

### Cross-Sections (Lower Panels)

Each cross-section has **two components**:

**Upper part (geophysical curves):**
- **ΔT (нТл)** — Total magnetic field anomaly (in nanoteslas), plotted as a continuous curve
- **Δg (мГл)** — Bouguer gravity anomaly (in milligals), plotted as a separate curve
- Values range approximately:
  - Magnetic: from approximately **-200 to +400 nT** (with sharp positive spikes reaching ~400 nT in section Z-N, suggesting shallow magnetic bodies or mafic/ultramafic intrusions)
  - Gravity: ranging approximately **-4 to +6 mGal**

**Lower part (geological interpretation panel):**
- Depth scale shown in **metres**, ranging from approximately **+2000 m (surface)** down to **-4000 m** (subsurface)
- Shows horizontally layered and deformed geological units with colour coding matching the map:
  - **Pink/magenta layers** — likely Paleozoic granitic basement or intrusive bodies
  - **Green layers** — possibly volcanic or specific sedimentary horizons
  - **Grey/beige layers** — sedimentary sequences
  - **Orange layers** — possibly Devonian volcanics
  - **Blue-green (teal) layer** — possibly a specific marker horizon (carbonate or volcanic)
- **Drill holes (скважины)** projected onto sections with depth indicators (e.g., Скв-428, Скв-635, and others partially legible)
- **Fault interpretations** shown as diagonal lines cutting through stratigraphy
- The cross-sections reveal a **basement high** in the central portion of lines Z-N and L-M, flanked by deeper sedimentary basins
- The **П-Р section** (bottom) shows more varied stratigraphy with multiple coloured units and several named localities along the surface profile

**Right-margin schema:**
- Small **index map** showing the position of sheet M-42-VIII relative to adjacent sheets (М-42, N-42 grid)
- Legend for map coverage units and survey types
- A table showing **площадь (area)** breakdown and possibly mineral occurrence data
